title: Pleasant Indian Fish Tikka Masala
description: A kid-friendly Indian dish featuring apricot jam and a cheesy twist, this pescatarian-friendly fish tikka masala is a citrusy delight.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together yogurt, lemon juice, and garam masala.
Add the cod fish pieces to the bowl and marinate for 10 minutes.
Heat the vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
Remove the fish from the marinade and cook in the skillet until browned on all sides.
Transfer the fish to a baking dish and top with apricot jam and shredded mozzarella cheese.
Bake in the preheated oven for 10 minutes, or until the cheese is melted and bubbly.
While the fish is baking, heat the chopped onion in the same skillet over medium heat until softened.
Serve the fish tikka masala hot, garnished with the sauteed onion.
